description: US FAA launch/reentry authorization precheck (US commercial flight scenarios). Research-grade snapshot; not legal advice.

rules.title: FAA launch and reentry authorization evidence (US commercial scenario)
rules.authority: Federal Aviation Administration, Office of Commercial Space Transportation
rules.source_reference: 51 U.S.C. chapter 509 (Commercial Space Launch Activities); 14 CFR Part 450 (Launch and Reentry License Requirements)
rules.finding_if_fail: No FAA Part 450 launch/reentry authorization application evidence is declared for a US commercial launch scenario.
rules.limitations: Precheck of declared evidence only. Whether the deorbit-kit-driven target reentry itself needs a Part 450 reentry authorization (vs. only the servicer launch) is an open legal question for ADR concepts - requires legal review.
rules.notes: Applicability is simplified to launching_state == 'US'; the statute also reaches US citizens/entities launching abroad.
